title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
content: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global pa

title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
content: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
content: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference

title: Russian hackers were behind $2.5 billion hack of Jaguar Land Rover: Report
content: The hack on car giant Jaguar Land Rover last year was one the most disrupting, damaging, and costly hacks of the last few years.

title: Tesla settles FSD crash lawsuit as federal investigations continue
content: The lawsuit was connected to a fatal 2023 crash involving a vehicle using the company's advanced driver assistance system known as Full Self-Driving.

title: TikTok’s road to becoming a super app
content: TikTok may be working to become the app that people use for most of their digital activities.

title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
content: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

title: Xprize founder says ‘humans behave better when they’re being watched’
content: Peter Diamandis is the latest tech executive to argue that global surveillance will make the world a better place, following Larry Ellison's comments in 2024.

title: Robotaxis drive miles just to get cleaned and charged; this new startup wants to fix that
content: Aseon Labs, which came out of Y Combinator's 2026 spring cohort, has raised $10 million from Crane Venture Partners and others.

title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
content: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.

title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
content: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

title: YouTube Shorts are getting even shorter with an update that lets you double the playback speed
content: YouTube Shorts is getting a makeover.

title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
content: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

title: Polymarket says hackers stole users’ funds
content: The prediction market giant Polymarket said it's refunding users who had funds stolen due to a third-party breach.

title: Xbox follows Apple with price increases
content: The company says the increases are being driven by rising memory and console storage prices, with costs more than 2.5x higher than previous levels.

title: Notion Mail shuts down amid agent takeover
content: The company said it is discontinuing its email inbox in favor of its AI agent offering as users are increasingly handing over the reins of their email to the agents.

title: a16z-backed Base Power is offering cheaper electricity to the power grid that needs it most
content: Base Power is skipping the PJM's troubled interconnection queue by placing its batteries at people's homes, offering backup services in exchange.

title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
content: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

title: General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
content: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

title: Databricks’ former AI chief thinks he can cut AI’s power bill by 1,000x
content: Un-0 is an image-generation system tool that shows for the first time how the company's technology can replicate conventional AI systems.
